level_signals: Comp range $180K-$205K base consistent with senior IC
Expected to own end-to-end roadmap and drive cross-functional alignment independently
Role described as critical and first-of-its-kind (no existing product owner)

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Strong experience working with Salesforce as a product surface including objects, flows, integrations, and GTM processes and the ability to partner deeply with Salesforce admins and engineers
Proven track record of integrating multiple internal systems (such as CRM, billing engines, data warehouses, and internal tools) rather than optimizing a single product in isolation
Comfortable working with APIs, backend services, and data pipelines, and able to make informed tradeoffs across these layers including querying and interpreting data using SQL or similar
Experience leading complex, cross-functional initiatives that span Engineering, GTM, Finance, and Operations, with multiple dependencies and stakeholders
Ability to run agile product delivery end-to-end, from backlog management and writing epics to release planning, with distributed engineering teams
Global experience with the flexibility to work as needed across flexible timezones including Europe and South America, with some travel required (<25%)

scope.surface: US Go-to-Market Platform: Salesforce, billing systems, partner residual tracking, internal tools, GTM reporting; consolidating legacy systems into unified global platform
primary_metrics_inferred: Legacy system retirement / platform consolidation progress
Quote-to-cash flow reliability and data trust
Reduction in operational burden across business teams
Roadmap delivery cadence and stakeholder alignment
core_responsibilities: Own and prioritize the US platform roadmap across Salesforce, billing systems, partner residual tracking, internal tools, and key reporting surfaces
Map and continuously refine the end-to-end quote-to-cash and reporting flow

Work closely with Platform Engineering, Data, Finance, Revenue Operations, and GTM leadership to understand pain points, define requirements, and balance short-term business needs with longer-term platform transformation
Translate data needs to Engineering and Analytics teams to streamline and bubble up business and product requirements
Produce and maintain a clear, shared map of all relevant US systems and flows
Define and run the rituals that keep delivery on track: intake, backlog grooming, sprint ceremonies, and regular stakeholder updates
Identify and lead quick wins alongside longer-term integration work

compensation.equity_form: Virtual Stock Option Program; Stock Option Plan participation

green_flags: Clear problem statement and scope — consolidating legacy systems with a defined end goal
New, greenfield role with genuine ownership and strategic impact
Generous L&D budget ($2,000/year), 22+ days PTO, 401(k) matching, sabbatical after 3 years
JD acknowledges candidates don't need to meet 100% of criteria
red_flags: No visa sponsorship available — eliminates candidates requiring work authorization
Office-first setup in Boulder, CO only — no remote option
Role is newly created with no existing processes or playbook — high ambiguity
Travel required (<25%) across Europe and South America timezones adds complexity
No explicit years-of-experience requirement stated — seniority expectations may be unclear during screening

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and role scope; not explicitly stated)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from 'Compensation will be finalized based on the candidate's experience and location')
ambiguities: Archetype sits between Technical PM and Technical Program Manager — the role involves owning a roadmap (PM) but also heavy coordination, rituals, and cross-functional orchestration (TPM). Classified as Technical Program Manager with secondary_archetype of Technical PM / Platform PM.
No years-of-experience minimum is stated anywhere in the JD; seniority inferred from comp and responsibilities only.
